description: Phase 1: Object inventory, dialect detection, complexity scoring

objects.name: ufn_AW19_SplitCsvInt
known_limitations: WHILE loop with manual string parsing (lines 54-62) — rewrite as F.split(col, ',') + F.explode() + .cast('int') with F.col().isNotNull() filter
TABLE variable @t — replace with DataFrame; no DDL needed in PySpark
TRY_CONVERT(INT, token) — PySpark .cast('int') already returns NULL on failure; behaviour is equivalent

objects.name: usp_AW19_SalesCommissionPricingPipeline
known_limitations: Correlated scalar subqueries in final SELECT at lines 579-586 (UniqueCustomers, AvgCustomerRFMScore) — pre-aggregate and flatten to LEFT JOINs before conversion
Temp table indexes with INCLUDE columns (lines 213-214, 255-257, 403-404) — no B-tree indexes in Spark; use .repartition(col).cache() for data locality
OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#X') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE checks — skip entirely; DataFrame lifecycle is auto-managed in PySpark
SELECT TOP (@TopNSalesPerson) … ORDER BY — convert to .orderBy(F.col('TotalAdjustedRevenue').desc()).limit(params.top_n_sales_person)
DATEDIFF(DAY, MAX(lf.OrderDate), @EndDate) at line 504 — argument order is REVERSED in PySpark: F.datediff(end_date, start_date)
SYSUTCDATETIME() — use F.current_timestamp() or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
BEGIN TRY … END CATCH at lines 160/594-600 — replace with Python try/except; add Delta Lake MERGE if ACID rollback is needed

Deeply nested 3-level CASE blocks (Discount %, Commission %, lines 323-396) — convert to chained F.when().when().otherwise() with care for NULL semantics and case-sensitive string comparisons (collation gotcha)
constructs_used: Clustered and non-clustered temp table indexes

objects.name: usp_AW19_ResolveTierOverride
known_limitations: OUTPUT parameter @ResolvedTier — return as a Python function return value or tuple element instead
String comparisons against @PersonType and @Title rely on SQL Server CI_AS collation — in PySpark use F.lower() or .ilike() to ensure case-insensitive matching
Scalar variable assignment via SELECT (lines 621-626) — rewrite as Python variables populated from df.first() on a filtered DataFrame

objects.name: usp_AW19_AuditPricingViolations
known_limitations: OUTPUT parameter @ViolationCount — return as Python function return value
OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#Violations') check at line 656 — skip; auto-managed in PySpark
Date range filter CAST(h.OrderDate AS DATE) BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate — use F.col('OrderDate').cast('date').between(start, end); verify no implicit type coercion issues

LEFT JOIN to dbo.AW19_PricingRules with date-range overlap condition (lines 690-694) — valid JOIN but relies on NULL handling for ExpiryDate; use F.col('ExpiryDate').isNull() | (F.col('ExpiryDate') >= end_date) in PySpark explicitly
